title: glyceria notata
intro: Glyceria notata, also known as fowl meadow-grass or small fowl meadow-grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Glyceria notata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural diuretic. Glyceria notata can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, often used topically to treat skin conditions or ingested to support overall health and well-being.
